perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, each party desires to provide and to receive information that may be consid- ered confidential or proprietary; and WHEREAS, prior to providing such information, each party requires a Non -Disclosure Agreement from the other party;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, each party desires to provide and to receive information that may be consid- ered confidential or proprietary; and WHEREAS, prior to providing such information, each party requires a Confidentiality Agreement from the other party;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' is replaced with 'Confidentiality Agreement'. While seemingly synonymous, the agreement is explicitly named 'NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' at the beginning. Changing the reference to 'Confidentiality Agreement' introduces ambiguity as to whether they are the same thing. This contradiction is immediately evident in the document's title versus the reference in this section, creating confusion about the agreement's actual name.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Page 1 of 5 LiION Mutual NDA.docx Form updated: 2.27.2015 NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT THIS NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“ Agreement ”) is made and entered into this _____ day of ___________, 20 15 (the “ Effective Date ”), by and between ______________ , a _________________________, having its principal place of business at ___________________ ___________________ (“ Vendor ”), and LiiON, LLC ., a Nevada limited liability corporation, having its principal place of business at 120 Prairie Lake Rd, Suite 1, East Dundee , IL 601 18 (“Company ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to a 'Non-Disclosure Agreement', which aligns with the document title 'NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces this with 'Confidentiality Agreement', creating a direct contradiction with the title and the reference within the contradicted section. This introduces ambiguity as to whether the agreement is indeed the 'NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' referenced elsewhere, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement: (a) “Affiliates ” mean s entities controlling, controlled by or under common control with Vendor or with Company.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement: (a) “Subsidiaries” means entities controlling, controlled by or under common control with Vendor or with Company.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Affiliates' with 'Subsidiaries' changes the scope of entities covered. 'Affiliates' is broader, potentially including entities with shared ownership but not necessarily direct control. 'Subsidiaries' implies a direct parent-subsidiary relationship. This contradicts section 2(c), where Affiliates are mentioned in relation to confidential information access. Therefore, it becomes ambigious if outside agents or affilates are the same as the new definition of Subsidiaries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Receiving Party may disclose the Con fidential Information to the employ- ees, officers, directors, and Affiliates of Receiving Party who need to know such Confi- dential Information in connection with the Purpose and who receive such information subject to the same or comparable restrictions as are contained in this Agreement as evi- denced by a signed non -disclosure agreement or equivalent . Disclosure of the Confiden- tial Information to outside agents and affiliates other than Receiving Party must be agreed upon by the Disclosing Party in writing .
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliates' broadly, while the changed text narrows the definition to 'Subsidiaries'. Section (c) of the contradicted text mentions 'Affiliates' regarding confidential information access and also mentions 'outside agents and affiliates'. The change to 'Subsidiaries' introduces ambiguity because the contradicted text makes a distinction between Affiliates and outside agents. Now, it becomes unclear if outside agents are included in Subsidiaries. Therefore, the change created a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (c) “Disclosing Party ” mean s the party disclosing the Confidential Infor- mation. (d) “Receiving Party ” mean s the party receiving the Confidential Infor- mation.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) “Disclosing Party ” means the party revealing the Confidential Information. (d) “Receiving Party ” means the party obtaining the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosing' to 'revealing' and 'receiving' to 'obtaining' adds ambiguity. While the intent is similar, 'revealing' and 'obtaining' imply a possibly unintentional disclosure or a taking of information, which differs from the more neutral 'disclosing' and 'receiving.' This in text contradiction leads to uncertainity when refering to the responsibilities laid out in Section 2, where the terms 'Receiving Party' and 'Disclosing Party' are mentioned
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information . (a) Receiving Party agrees that it will only use Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the extent necessary for the Purpose. (b) Except a s provided in this Agreement, Receiving Party shall not intention- ally disclose to any other person, firm or corporation or use for its own benefit any Confi- dential Information it receives from Disclosing Party. (c) Receiving Party may disclose the Con fidential Information to the employ- ees, officers, directors, and Affiliates of Receiving Party who need to know such Confi- dential Information in connection with the Purpose and who receive such information subject to the same or comparable restrictions as are contained in this Agreement as evi- denced by a signed non -disclosure agreement or equivalent . Disclosure of the Confiden- tial Information to outside agents and affiliates other than Receiving Party must be agreed upon by the Disclosing Party in writing .. (d) Each party shall use at least the same degree of care, but in no event less than reasonable care, to avoid inadvertent disclosure or unpermitted use of the other par- ty's Confidential Information which it employs with respect to its own proprietar y or con- fidential information of a similar nature which it does not wish to have disseminated, published or disclosed. (e) This Agreement shall not be construed to bind or impose obligations upon any other division, subsidiaries or business units of Receiving Party or its Affiliates , ex- cept for any such divisions , subsidiaries, business units or Affiliates that have access to the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Disclosing Party' as the party disclosing and 'Receiving Party' as the party receiving. The changed text alters these to 'revealing' and 'obtaining' respectively. While seemingly similar, 'revealing' and 'obtaining' introduce ambiguity regarding intentionality, potentially implying unintentional disclosure or a forceful acquisition of information, differing from the neutral 'disclosing' and 'receiving.' Section 2 outlines the 'Receiving Party' responsibilities concerning the 'Disclosing Party's' Confidential Information, assuming a clear, intentional transfer. The change weakens this clarity, as 'revealing' could imply accidental disclosure and 'obtaining' could mean acquiring information without explicit consent, thus contradicting the expected intentionality within Section 2 and creating ambiguity in how the receiving party should act based on the terms.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Except a s provided in this Agreement, Receiving Party shall not intention- ally disclose to any other person, firm or corporation or use for its own benefit any Confi- dential Information it receives from Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Except as provided in this Agreement, Receiving Party shall not intention- ally reveal to any other person, firm or corporation or use for its own benefit any Proprietary Information it receives from Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' in this section causes a contradiction. The agreement defines 'Confidential Information' in Section 1(b), encompassing marked information and trade secrets. Switching to 'Proprietary Information', which is not defined, introduces uncertainty. Is all 'Confidential Information' also 'Proprietary Information,' or vice-versa? It now becomes ambigious to what information the Receiving Party's responsibilities apply to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) “Confidential Information ” mean s: (1) any information, whether tangi- ble or intangible, in written or in machine readable form that is mar ked or designated in writing as “Proprietary” or “Confidential” at the time of disclosure ; (2) any information disclosed orally or visually to Receiving P arty, provided that such information is orally identified as “Confidential” or “Proprietary” prior to or at the time of its disclosure , (3) any information derived or extracted from information provided to Receiving Party that Receiving Party reasonably should conclude was intended to be confidential, or (4) any information that is considered a trade secre t and not expressly released to Receiving Party by Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information' which is explicitly defined in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces this with 'Proprietary Information', which is not defined. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' to include information marked 'Proprietary' or 'Confidential'. By using 'Proprietary Information' instead of 'Confidential Information', the altered text introduces ambiguity regarding which information the Receiving Party's responsibilities apply to, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within 15 days of written request from Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall promptly return Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof to Disclosing Party or destroy Disclosing Party’s Conf idential Information, in- cluding all copies thereof, purge its computer systems of such Confidential Information and cer- tify such destruction in writing signed by a duly authorized representative .
perturbation.changed_text: Within 15 days of written request from Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall promptly return Disclosing Party’s Sensitive Information and all copies thereof to Disclosing Party or destroy Disclosing Party’s Sensitive Information, in- cluding all copies thereof, purge its computer systems of such Sensitive Information and cer- tify such destruction in writing signed by a duly authorized representative .
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' creates confusion because 'Sensitive Information' is never defined. It's unclear if 'Sensitive Information' has the same scope as defined 'Confidential Information', impacting the Receiving Party's obligations. This creates contradiction, especially when the purpose of the agreement is clearly on ensuring protection of 'Confidential Information'. Now it is unknown, if 'Sensitive Information' falls under the category of the definition under Section 1(b)
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) “Confidential Information ” mean s: (1) any information, whether tangi- ble or intangible, in written or in machine readable form that is mar ked or designated in writing as “Proprietary” or “Confidential” at the time of disclosure ; (2) any information disclosed orally or visually to Receiving P arty, provided that such information is orally identified as “Confidential” or “Proprietary” prior to or at the time of its disclosure , (3) any information derived or extracted from information provided to Receiving Party that Receiving Party reasonably should conclude was intended to be confidential, or (4) any information that is considered a trade secre t and not expressly released to Receiving Party by Disclosing Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information'. The contradicted text provides a specific definition for 'Confidential Information'. Because 'Sensitive Information' is not defined, it's unclear if the obligations related to 'Confidential Information' now apply to 'Sensitive Information', creating a contradiction and potential unenforceability because the Receiving Party's obligations are now uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: (g) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modifi- cation of this Agreement shall be in writing and executed by duly authorized representa- tives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) This Contract constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modifi- cation of this Contract shall be in writing and executed by duly authorized representa- tives of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' within the same sentence creates confusion. While both terms are often used interchangeably, the document consistently refers to itself as an 'Agreement'. Switching to 'Contract' implies a distinction, suggesting that there might be a 'Contract' separate from the mentioned 'Agreement' without defining the relationship. The contradiction is direct, as it refers to the same legal document, yet names it differently within the same clause, and makes it ambigious what document the 'subject matter' refers to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) This Contract constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. Any amendment or modifi- cation of this Contract shall be in writing and executed by duly authorized representa- tives of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Agreement' to 'Contract' within the same sentence, while the document consistently refers to itself as an 'Agreement'. This creates a Misaligned Terminology- In Text Contradiction (as defined above). It introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, because the 'subject matter' could refer to the Agreement or the Contract, as explained above. The Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text because both texts are identical, and the change to the Original Text has introduced a contradiction in the document, as explained above. Therefore, based on the explanation given, there is a clear and direct contradiction within the document.
